message:
fix(raptorq): conservative tie-breaker in decision contract, DRY test fixtures in gf256

Two raptorq improvements:

1. **Decision contract tie-breaker (decision_contract.rs)**: G7's
   choose_action() previously used bayes_action() which could
   select any minimum-loss action non-deterministically on ties.
   Now uses an explicit deterministic tie-breaker that prefers
   conservative actions: fallback > rollback > canary_hold >
   continue (implemented via reverse index comparison in
   then_with).  This ensures that when two actions have equal
   expected loss, the safer action is always chosen.
   Test: choose_action_uses_conservative_tie_breaker verifies that
   equal expected loss between continue and canary_hold selects
   canary_hold.

2. **GF256 test fixture DRY (gf256.rs)**: Extracted
   x86_profile_pack_policy_fixture() helper that builds a
   canonical DualKernelPolicy from X86Avx2BalancedV1 metadata,
   replacing 4 inline policy constructions that duplicated 15+
   constant references each.  Test scenarios now derive from the
   fixture's live fields (base.addmul_max_total, base.addmul_min_lane)
   instead of hard-coded numeric literals, making them resilient
   to tuning parameter changes.  DualKernelPolicySnapshot test
   similarly updated to use metadata accessors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
